notes: wkr-016-003 and wkr-016-004 returned platform 429 (too many requests); analysis completed manually from repo + run artifacts.

update_mechanism.summary: swarm-state.json updates are event-driven and primarily written by the commander agent; the monitor never writes it and does not compute counters from filesystem state.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh:155-189 reads telemetry fields to display counts; no writes
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md:57 requires commander to update swarm-state.json after child launches
bin/stampede.sh:830 seeds swarm-state.json once at commander registration; other launcher writes only on blocked/launch-failed paths

atomicity.summary: Launcher uses atomic tmp->replace in some paths, but initial seed write is non-atomic; commander live-update atomicity is unspecified by contract.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh:830-831 initial seed uses open(...,'w') + json.dump (non-atomic)
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md:38 only mandates python json.dump; does not mandate tmp+rename for swarm-state.json updates

heartbeat_tracking.summary: Heartbeat is derived from last_heartbeat_at (fallback updated_at). Missing periodic writer means heartbeats only advance when the commander writes state. Monitor considers commander inactive if heartbeat age >= 180s.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh:167-173 parses last_heartbeat_at/updated_at and displays hb age
bin/stampede-monitor.sh:25 sets ORPHAN_RECOVERY_AFTER=180; lines 247-276 requeue on stale heartbeat + no live panes
Current commander-005 swarm-state.json has updated_at == last_heartbeat_at == 2026-05-01T01:13:18Z

running_completed_counters.summary: Squad-lead counters in swarm-state.json are internally consistent and match the child-agents ledger, but worker counters are not being updated at all in this run.
evidence: .stampede/run-20260430-180646/commanders/commander-005/child-agents.jsonl contains only squad_lead launch_started + completed; no worker events

failed_counter_handling.summary: Monitor displays failed = workers_failed (fallback children_failed). Neither is computed from ledger/filesystem; failures must be explicitly written by commander. Launcher only marks failed if commander CLI launch fails.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh:985-1011 updates swarm-state.json on commander launch failure (copilot_launch_failed); no per-child failure aggregation
failed_counter_handling.risk: If workers/squad-leads fail but commander doesn’t increment workers_failed/children_failed, monitor will show 0 failures and may misreport run health.

atoms_received_tracking.summary: atoms_received is currently not tracking reality. Monitor trusts telemetry.atoms_received and will not count atoms/ directory contents.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh:163-166 sets completed from workers_completed fallback atoms_received and prints atoms count
bin/stampede-monitor.sh:27-30 has count_json_files() but is not used to derive atoms_received
commander-005 swarm-state.json has telemetry.atoms_received=0 while commander-005/atoms has 19 json files
atoms_received_tracking.impact: Nested progress line will under-report completion/atoms and can trigger '⚠ incomplete proof' warnings even when atoms exist.

recommendations: Commander: whenever writing an atom file into atoms/, increment telemetry.atoms_received and set workers_completed accordingly; update swarm-state.json + append completed event with atom_id to child-agents.jsonl.
Commander: update swarm-state.json with tmp+replace (atomic) on every heartbeat/update to avoid partial reads.
Monitor: add defensive fallback counts (if telemetry missing/zero) by counting atoms/*.json and/or parsing child-agents.jsonl completed events.
Telemetry contract: explicitly require launch_requested events (currently absent in this run) so the parent monitor can prove intent vs. actual launch_started.
